mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher over the subsequent 20 trading days through elevated earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2012 payroll vintage shows 134,021 thousand persons employed
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k jobs.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% for more than 6 consecutive months, the zero lower bound constraint signals extended monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and inflates equity risk premiums, producing negative forward returns as the market reprices duration risk over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:December 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.16%, indicating ZLB regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC reverses sign and becomes significantly negative (< -0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance revisions cascade through earnings estimates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:November 2012 HOUST at 861k units, RSAFS at 415.7B dollars
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month indicates accelerating supply-side capacity utilization that signals pricing power and margin expansion, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices profit growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth INDPRO months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate hiring expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premiums widen.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC magnitude falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.50% AND CPIAUCSL inflation below 2.0% year-over-year signals that real monetary policy is deeply accommodative with no inflation constraint, enabling equity valuations to expand through lower discount rates and extended duration premiums over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 48% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.12) in rolling 1-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations in the expansion regime.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 800k annualized units AND a contraction in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a demand-side shock that compresses equity valuations through lower earnings forecasts and elevated recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices tail risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 800k annualized AND GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:November 2012 HOUST 861k units, Q2 2012 GDPC1 growth subdued
regime_hint: demand-side shock or early recession signal
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 54% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15) in rolling 1-year regressions.
